title: charness t-events adapter
description: Repo-self-config manifest declaring how charness should emit T-loop events into this consumer repo. Loaded from .agents/t-events-adapter.yaml. T-events are the artifact-level analog of hermes-agent runtime telemetry: charness skills emit lifecycle records that downstream skills (e.g., the Skill-T mechanism inventory) consume to populate Tier C evidence. Emit is opt-in per consumer; absence of this manifest means events are not captured for this repo.

repo.description: Optional consumer repo identifier. Reserved for future cross-repo aggregation; v1 ignores it.

enabled.description: Whether charness skills should emit T-events for this consumer. When false, the manifest still parses cleanly but emit is suppressed.

storage_path.description: Path (relative to consumer repo root) where charness appends event records. Each event type writes to <storage_path>/<event_type>.jsonl with one JSON object per line.

events.description: Subset of v1 event types this consumer captures. When absent, all v1 events are captured.

max_files.description: Maximum rotated files to retain per event type. Older files are deleted. Absent means unbounded.

max_size_mb.description: Rotation threshold in MB. When the active jsonl file exceeds this size, charness rotates it.

rotation.description: Optional retention policy for emitted records. Absent fields mean no cap.
